name: City Tour of Málaga - Circular Walk
name_original: City tour por Málaga – paseo circular
region_intro: Road cycling around La Axarquía features diverse landscapes, transitioning from 30 miles of Mediterranean coastline to rugged mountain ranges inland. The region is characterized by rolling hills adorned with olive groves and vineyards, alongside towering limestone peaks and dense pine forests. Numerous white villages are nestled throughout the terrain, providing scenic backdrops for routes. Best road cycling routes around La Axarquía The most popular road cycling route is Puerto de León y Puerto de la Bolina — circular route from Málaga, a 30.7 miles (49.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain through mountain passes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Torrox and the Axarquía White Villages Trail, a difficult 29.7 miles (47.8 km) path. This route blends coastal sections with climbs through the region's traditional white villages. Local road cyclists also love the Nerja, Frigiliana, and Torrox Loop, a 20.6 miles (33.2 km) trail leading through coastal towns and the white village of Frigiliana, often completed in about 1 hour 46 minutes. Road cycling around La Axarquía is defined by its Mediterranean coastline, varied mountain ranges, and charming white villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in La Axarquía are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 800 reviews. More than 44,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore La Axarquía's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in La Axarquía?
A: La Axarquía offers a wide variety of road cycling routes, with over 340 options available. These routes cater to different skill levels, including approximately 70 easy, 135 moderate, and 135 difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in La Axarquía?
A: The region features incredibly diverse terrain, transitioning from 30 kilometers of Mediterranean coastline to rugged mountain ranges inland. You'll encounter rolling hills covered in olive groves, almond trees, vineyards, and tropical fruit plantations, as well as towering limestone peaks and dense pine forests. This variety ensures routes for all preferences, from coastal flats to challenging mountain passes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in La Axarquía?
A: La Axarquía boasts an excellent climate for cycling year-round, with around 320 days of sunshine annually. Temperatures remain pleasant even in winter, making it an ideal destination for road cycling during any season.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that pass through the famous white villages of La Axarquía?
A: Yes, many routes wind through the charming white villages (Pueblos Blancos) of Moorish origin, offering picturesque backdrops and cultural immersion. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937175700/torrox-and-the-axarquia-white-villages-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrox and the Axarquía White Villages Trail&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937051503/nerja-frigiliana-and-torrox-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerja, Frigiliana, and Torrox Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932540417/axarquia-white-villages-loop-from-torre-del-mar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Axarquía White Villages Loop from Torre del Mar&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices for experiencing these unique towns.

Q: Can I find road cycling routes with panoramic views of the coast or mountains?
A: Absolutely. La Axarquía is renowned for its breathtaking panoramic views. Many routes offer stunning vistas of the Mediterranean Sea, the dramatic mountain ranges, and the lush agricultural landscapes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932911429/climb-to-olias-with-panoramic-views-of-montes-de-malaga-circular-from-torre-del-mar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Olías with panoramic views of Montes de Málaga&lt;/a&gt; provides exceptional scenery.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that include Lake Viñuela?
A: Yes, Lake Viñuela, a scenic reservoir, is a popular feature on several cycling routes. You can explore the area around the lake on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939641218/embalse-de-la-vinuela-circular-from-periana-por-el-puerto-sabar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de La Viñuela – circular from Periana por el Puerto Sábar&lt;/a&gt;, which offers beautiful views of the water and surrounding pine trees.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions I might see while cycling in La Axarquía?
A: Beyond the stunning landscapes, you can encounter several natural attractions. The region is bordered by the Natural Parks of Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama and Montes de Málaga. You might also cycle near the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943784017/natural-site-cliffs-of-maro-cerro-gordo-via-eurovelo-8" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of Maro-Cerro Gordo&lt;/a&gt; along the coast, or pass by the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/393620" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Viñuela Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in the region?
A: Yes, La Axarquía offers numerous circular road cycling routes, making it convenient to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930497446/puerto-de-leon-y-puerto-de-la-bolina-circular-route-from-malaga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de León y Puerto de la Bolina — circular route from Málaga&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930351076/visit-the-alcazaba-of-velez-malaga-and-villages-of-la-axarquia-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit the Alcazaba of Vélez-Málaga and villages of La Axarquía – circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in La Axarquía?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the well-paved roads with low-to-medium traffic, and the opportunity to combine challenging climbs with visits to charming white villages and coastal views.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, La Axarquía offers demanding routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931340064/el-torcal-loop-from-malaga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Torcal Loop from Málaga&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a difficult route covering nearly 90 km with over 2,100 meters of climbing, providing a rigorous test for fitness.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Montes de Málaga Natural Park?
A: Yes, the Montes de Málaga Natural Park, known for its extensive pine, cypress, and oak forests, is accessible via road cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930497446/puerto-de-leon-y-puerto-de-la-bolina-circular-route-from-malaga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de León y Puerto de la Bolina — circular route from Málaga&lt;/a&gt; is one such route that takes you through this beautiful natural area.
tips: The Carril Bici Junto al Mar is a cycle path that runs along the coast in Málaga. The path offers a beautiful route for cyclists who enjoy the view of the sea and the beaches while cycling. Of course, you should always watch out for bathers and pedestrians.

Right between the Parque de Málaga (botanical park in the middle of the city) and the Palmeral de las Sorpresas (port of Málaga), a good place to stop and rest for a while in the shade of the exotic plants. Paseo de los Curas has a lane for bikes and scooters. Arriving at Muelle Uno, on the right, you can see "El Cubo", a futuristic colored glass construction - it is the Pompidou Center of Malaga, museum of modern art.

The lighthouse in Malaga, known as La Farola de Malaga, is an iconic landmark of the Andalusian port city. It is located at the port of Malaga and is one of the few lighthouses in Spain named in the feminine form. “Farola” means “The Lighthouse Woman”. La Farola was built in 1817 and has served as a landmark for ships heading to the port ever since. Over the years, the lighthouse has been modernized to meet the demands of modern shipping. It stands on the Paseo de la Farola, a popular promenade that runs along the port. In addition to its practical function, the Farola also has symbolic value and represents the maritime spirit of Malaga. 

The lighthouse itself is about 38 meters high and has a very characteristic cylindrical shape, making it an unmistakable element of the Malaga skyline. Especially at sunset, the area around the lighthouse offers beautiful views of the sea and the city. A great destination for a tour in Malaga.
One way street uphill from Paseo de Sancha up into the hills. You can go down on the other side of the rarely used but absolutely necessary Arroyo Toquero when it rains.

Small and quite quiet street through the Parque Forestal Monte Victoria. A real surprise, because almost in the middle of the city you suddenly drive right through the forest. Being close to the city, a car always comes along even though you don't expect it.

waypoints.name: Winding Road Through Parque Forestal Monte Victoria
